metaDescription: The tools I use daily for carpentry, cabinet making, and woodworking. Honest recommendations from a working carpenter.
title: My Carpentry
intro: Every tool here is something I personally use and recommend. No fluff — just the gear that makes my work better.
disclosure: 🔗 Some links are affiliate links. I earn a small commission if you buy through them — at no extra cost to you. This helps support my content.

tools.name: Makita Track Saw
tools.description: My go-to for clean, straight cuts on sheet materials. Essential for cabinet work.

tools.name: Festool Domino Joiner
tools.description: Game-changer for strong, fast joinery. Worth every penny.

tools.name: Makita Cordless Drill
tools.description: Reliable everyday driver. I own three of these.

tools.name: Makita Cordless Jigsaw
tools.description: For curved cuts and quick scribing on site.

tools.name: Stanley FatMax Tape Measure 5m
tools.description: The yellow tape you see in every photo. Accurate and durable.

tools.name: Irwin Marples Chisels
tools.description: Great edge retention, comfortable grip. Use them daily.

tools.name: Bahco Hand Saw
tools.description: For quick cuts where setting up a power tool isn't worth it.

tools.name: Stabila Spirit Level
tools.description: Accurate and built to survive a building site.

tools.name: Bosch Laser Measure
tools.description: Instant room measurements. Saves hours on site surveys.

tools.name: Shinwa Combination Square
tools.description: Precision marking for joinery and cabinet work.

tools.name: Incra T-Rule
tools.description: Dead accurate for marking out cuts on boards.

tools.name: SketchUp Pro
tools.description: I design every project in 3D before building. Non-negotiable.

tools.name: Cabinetos
tools.description: My own app — automatic cut lists, parts lists, and board optimisation.

footerCtaSubtitle: I write about tools and techniques on my blog. Check it out for honest reviews and tips.
